description_long: This object is a compact, intricate mechanical clock movement, featuring a dense arrangement of interlocking gears, cogs, and jewel bearings. The main body is constructed from silvery metal plates, with visible screws and several purple synthetic jewels set into the surface, which serve as low-friction pivot points for the gears. A winding stem with a black knurled crown protrudes from one side, used for setting the time and winding the mechanism. The exposed gears are made of brass and steel, with some components showing fine machining and precision engineering. The overall form is flat and rounded, typical of a watch or small clock movement, with no visible clock face or numerals. The back side reveals additional gearwork and mounting holes, emphasizing its function as an internal mechanism rather than a standalone timepiece.
description: A compact, metallic mechanical clock movement with exposed gears, jewel bearings, and a winding stem, designed for internal use in a clock or watch.
description_view_0: This view highlights the top plate with visible gears, jewel bearings, and the winding stem partially visible at the top.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the thickness of the movement, the protruding winding stem, and the layered construction of the plates.
description_view_2: This edge view shows the narrow profile, gear teeth, and the winding mechanism components from the side.
description_view_3: This view provides a clear look at the main gear train, jewel settings, and the full winding stem, offering the most complete view of the mechanism's layout.
attribute_face_design: No visible clock face; only the internal mechanism with gears, screws, and jewel bearings is exposed.
